During my time, I was part of the 27th, 31st, and 34th training battalions on the West Coast. The Marine Corps shaped my early adult life and gave me a sense of purpose.

implicit_text: I didn't want to join the naval service, so I chose the amphibious infantry instead. However, I did encounter a shore patrol sailor while returning from furlough, who helped me with traveler's orders when I was three days late getting back. The naval shore patrol played a role in maintaining order among servicemen during that time.
explicit_text: I didn't want to join the United States Navy, so I chose the Marine Corps instead. However, I did encounter a shore patrol sailor while returning from furlough, who helped me with traveler's orders when I was three days late getting back. The Navy's shore patrol played a role in maintaining order among servicemen during that time.

implicit_text: Arnie Martin was a kid I used to play ball with, and we joined the tough amphibious infantry together in 1944. While I was sent to San Diego for boot camp, Arnie was sent to the East Coast base at Camp Lejeune and then to another recruit depot at Parris Island. We started this journey together, but our paths diverged based on the assignments within that branch.
